## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/slides/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → FigJam file, use get_figjam; pass the original board URL as figjamUrl when available
- figma.com/slides/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → Figma Slides file

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the t… [truncated]

attachment.content:
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- diff-review: Explain an implementation diff and produce a prioritized code review checklist. Use when the user asks for /diff-review, local code review, diff explanation, or what reviewers should inspect first.
- review: Multi-persona code review — Linus, Fowler, and Vogels each review a PR and have a discussion round. Uses local Claude token. Run as /review <PR_NUMBER>.
- fastapi: FastAPI best practices — project structure, route patterns, Pydantic v2, dependency injection, settings, error handling. References: fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template, benavlabs/FastAPI-boilerplate, zhanymkanov/fastapi-best-practices, fastapi-practices/fastapi_best_architecture.
- changelog-generator: Automatically creates user-facing changelogs from git commits by analyzing commit history, categorizing changes, and transforming technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Turns hours of manual changelog writing into minutes of automated generation.
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
- feature: Interactive feature workflow — gather requirements interactively (description, API list, branch name, context), then run design → implement → PR in sequence. Use when the user wants to build a new feature end-to-end.
- plan-for-codex: Analyze the user's request and write a PLAN.md at the worktree root that Codex (running in another tab on the same worktree) can read and implement. Do not modify any code yourself — only produce the plan. Use when the user wants Claude to design and Codex to implement.
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- assignment: End-to-end assignment workflow — loads tech stack constraints → /think (design, 7 stages) → /iterate (Codex implements) → README polish → final gate. Usage: /assignment <assignment description or path>
- diff-review: Diff Review Brief
- iterate: Multi-agent iteration loop with human checkpoints — Pre-build → Builder → Spot-check → Tester → Reviewer → Human. Usage: /iterate <feature description>
- code-review: Multi-axis code review across correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use before merging any change — from yourself, another agent, or a human. Usage: /code-review [PR# | git ref | empty for current diff]
- plan: Design Agent — 3-phase collaborative design with human checkpoints at each stage. Outputs REQUIREMENTS.md + PLAN.md. Usage: /plan
- design: Analyze requirements and create a design spec before implementation. Writes to /tmp/spec.md and waits for user approval.
- think: Design thinking partner — walks alongside you through 7-stage expert planning. Shows the internal monologue of a senior engineer at each stage, then asks sharp questions to sharpen your thinking. Usage: /think <problem or decision>
- assignment: Execute the Agentic IDE take-home assignment playbook
- evaluate: Score a submission against the IC1 Backend 5-axis rubric (25 points). Usage: /evaluate [path-to-submission]
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

message.content:
이 내용을 기반으로 @case-01-session-second/ 파일 수정해주고 /assignment 도 보완해줘 
second가 더 잘하려면 핵심은 세 가지였습니다.

  1. 명세 endpoint를 그대로 열었어야 함
     과제는 POST /sessions/{id}/patches/{patchId}/check를 요구했는데, second는 case-01-session-second/src/
     routes.py:40에서 POST /patches/{patch_id}/check로 바꿨습니다. 설계상 합리적일 수는 있지만 채점자는 “요구
     endpoint가 있나?”부터 봅니다. 더 좋은 해법은 자기 설계를 유지하더라도 명세 endpoint를 alias로 같이 제공
     하는 겁니다.
  2. E2E 데모가 R4/R5 BLOCK을 증명했어야 함
     second의 mock patch는 case-01-session-second/src/llm.py:103에서 def generated_change(): pass라서 전체
     workflow 테스트가 “위험 diff를 만들고 guardrail이 막는다”를 보여주지 못합니다. R4/R5 unit test는 있지만,
     과제의 대표 시나리오는 print()와 requests.*가 실제 /check에서 BLOCK 되는 것입니다.
  3. patch 생성 LLM에도 AGENTS.md를 넣었어야 함
     second는 plan prompt에는 AGENTS.md를 넣지만 case-01-session-second/src/llm.py:85의 patch prompt에는
     brand rule context가 없습니다. 실제 제품 관점에서는 “LLM이 브랜드 규칙을 알고 patch를 만들고,
     deterministic guardrail이 다시 검증한다”가 더 강한 구조입니다.

  backend의 README/데모가 더 좋다고 한 이유는 이겁니다.

  - README가 과제 평가자가 보고 싶은 framing을 더 잘 잡았습니다. “Cursor clone이 아니라 DH-aware integration
    layer”, “LLM proposes; deterministic checks decide”, “Response Shape = Workflow Signal” 같은 설명이 제품
    의도를 잘 드러냅니다.
  - 데모 시나리오가 과제 샘플에 더 가깝습니다. backend의 mock diff는 case-01-session-backend/src/llm.py:68에
    서 print()와 requests.get()를 포함하고, E2E도 R4/R5가 BLOCK 되는지 직접 확인합니다.
  - README의 curl workflow도 “세션 생성 → plan → patch → check → full state” 흐름이 채점자 입장에서 바로 따라
    가기 쉽습니다.

  정리하면: backend는 포장과 데모 스토리가 좋고, second는 실제 코드 구조와 테스트 범위가 더 좋습니다. second
  가 endpoint 명세와 R4/R5 데모까지 맞췄으면 훨씬 확실한 승리였습니다.


› second가 만점을 받기 위해서는 뭐가 필요해?


• second가 만점에 가까워지려면 “좋은 구조”는 이미 있으니, 남은 건 명세 일치성과 평가자 설득력입니다.

  1. 명세 endpoint를 그대로 지원
     현재:
     POST /patches/{patch_id}/check

     추가 필요:
     POST /sessions/{session_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check

     세션 ID와 patch ID가 서로 맞는지도 검증하면 더 좋습니다. 기존 짧은 endpoint는 남겨도 되지만, 과제 요구
     endpoint는 반드시 있어야 합니다.
  2. 과제 샘플 diff를 E2E로 검증
     테스트에 다음 시나리오가 있어야 합니다.
      - from .utils import calc
      - public function + docstring
      - print(...)
      - requests.get(...)

     그리고 /check 결과에서:
      - R4 = BLOCK/fail
      - R5 = BLOCK/fail
      - R3 = pass
      - R1은 과제 본문에 “passes R1”이라고 되어 있지만 실제 from .utils는 상대 import라 모순입니다. 이 모순을
        README에 명시해야 합니다.
  3. LLM patch prompt에도 AGENTS.md 주입
     지금은 plan에는 brand guidelines가 들어가지만 patch 생성에는 안 들어갑니다. 만점권이라면 patch 생성도
     “브랜드 규칙을 보고 생성”하고, guardrail이 다시 deterministic하게 검증하는 구조가 더 강합니다.
  4. AGENTS.md 파싱을 더 명확하게
     지금 second는 severity만 AGENTS.md에서 파싱합니다. 만점권이면 “룰 정의 파일을 consult한다”는 걸 더 분명
     히 보여줘야 합니다.

     예:
      - BrandPolicy 같은 객체로 efood/AGENTS.md를 읽음
      - R1-R5가 없으면 서버 시작/체크 시 fail-fast
      - README에 “룰 의미는 deterministic implementation, severity/source는 AGENTS.md”처럼 한계 명시
  5. README를 backend 수준으로 다듬기
     README에 다음이 더 분명히 있어야 합니다.
      - 실제 endpoint가 과제 endpoint와 1:1 매핑된 표
      - “LLM proposes; deterministic guardrails decide” 구조 설명
      - 과제 모순: from .utils가 R1 passes라고 쓰였지만 AGENTS.md상 relative import라 fail 처리한다는 결정
      - AI 사용 내역과 사람이 검증한 부분
      - 하지 않은 것: auth, persistence, queue, multi-brand full routing, OTEL export
  6. 테스트 보강
     만점권 테스트는 최소 이렇게 봅니다.
      - 모든 과제 endpoint smoke test
      - R4/R5 sample diff E2E
      - missing session/patch 404
      - patch가 session에 속하지 않으면 404 또는 409
      - AGENTS.md missing/malformed 처리
      - mock LLM 없이도 deterministic test 가능
      - ruff check, ruff format --check, pytest
  7. 보안/운영 감각 한 줄 추가
     README에 다음 정도만 있어도 점수가 올라갑니다.
      - API key는 env로만 받음
      - LLM에는 session title/description/target files/AGENTS.md만 보냄
      - 전체 repo나 secrets는 보내지 않음
      - trace_id는 후속 OTEL 연결용

  결론: second가 만점 받으려면 큰 리팩터링보다 명세 endpoint 복구 + sample diff E2E + README 보강 + patch
  prompt AGENTS.md 주입이 가장 중요합니다. 지금 85점짜리라면, 이 네 개만 해도 94~97점권입니다.

35	    session = _require_session(session_id)
36	    step_inputs = llm.create_plan(session.title, session.description, session.brand)
37	    steps = [_to_plan_step(step) for step in step_inputs]
38	    store.save_steps(session_id, steps)
39	    return [PlanStepOut.model_validate(step) for step in steps]

42	def create_patch(session_id: UUID, payload: PatchCreate) -> PatchProposalOut:
43	    session = _require_session(session_id)
44	    step = store.find_step(session_id, payload.step_id)
45	    if step is None:
46	        raise NotFoundError("step not found")
47	    patch_input = llm.create_patch(_to_plan_step_input(step))
48	    patch = _to_patch(session, payload.step_id, patch_input.diff)
49	    stored = store.save_patch(session_id, patch)
50	    if stored is None:

60	    stored = store.save_checks(patch_id, checks)

53	    return PatchProposalInput.model_validate(result)
54

56	def _call_tool(tool: dict[str, Any], prompt: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
57	    client = Anthropic()
58	    response = client.messages.create(
59	        model=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_MODEL", "claude-sonnet-4-5"),
60	        max_tokens=4096,
61	        tools=[tool],
62	        tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "output"},
63	        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
64	    )
65	    return response.content[0].input

69	    path = Path(f"{brand}/AGENTS.md")
70	    return path.read_text() if path.exists() else ""
71

74	    agents = _read_agents(brand)
75	    agents_section = f"Brand guidelines:\n{agents}\n" if agents else ""
76	    return (

78	        f"Title: {title}\n"
79	        f"Description: {description}\n"
80	        f"{agents_section}"
81	        "Return only the output tool payload."
82	    )
83	
84	
85	def _patch_prompt(step: PlanStepInput) -> str:
86	    return (
87	        "Create a unified diff for this implementation step.\n"
88	        f"Description: {step.description}\n"
89	        f"Target files: {', '.join(step.target_files)}\n"

1	import re
2	from collections.abc import Callable
3	from pathlib import Path
4	
5	from src.models import Brand, GuardrailCheck
6	
7	RuleMatcher = Callable[[list[str]], bool]
8	
9	_FALLBACK_SEVERITY: dict[str, str] = {
10	    "R1": "WARN",
11	    "R2": "BLOCK",
12	    "R3": "WARN",
13	    "R4": "BLOCK",
14	    "R5": "BLOCK",
15	}
16	
17	
18	def run_checks(diff: str, brand: Brand) -> list[GuardrailCheck]:
19	    added = _added_lines(diff)
20	    sev = _parse_severities(brand)
21	    return [
22	        _check("R1", sev.get("R1", "WARN"), _has_relative_import, added),
23	        _check("R2", sev.get("R2", "BLOCK"), _has_shell_execution, added),
24	        _check("R3", sev.get("R3", "WARN"), _has_public_function_without_docstring, added),
25	        _check("R4", sev.get("R4", "BLOCK"), _has_print_call, added),
26	        _check("R5", sev.get("R5", "BLOCK"), _has_requests_call, added),
27	    ]
28	
29	
30	def _parse_severities(brand: Brand) -> dict[str, str]:
31	    try:
32	        text = Path(f"{brand}/AGENTS.md").read_text()
33	    except FileNotFoundError:
34	        return _FALLBACK_SEVERITY.copy()
35	    pattern = re.compile(r"-\s+(R\d+)\s+(WARN|BLOCK|INFO):")
36	    parsed = {m.group(1): m.group(2) for m in pattern.finditer(text)}
37	    return {**_FALLBACK_SEVERITY, **parsed}
38	
39	
40	def _added_lines(diff: str) -> list[str]:
41	    return [
42	        line[1:]
43	        for line in diff.splitlines()
44	        if line.startswith("+") and not line.startswith("+++")
45	    ]
46	
47	
48	def _check(
49	    rule_id: str,
50	    severity: str,
51	    matcher: RuleMatcher,
52	    added: list[str],
53	) -> GuardrailCheck:
54	    result = "fail" if matcher(added) else "pass"
55	    return GuardrailCheck(
56	        ruleId=rule_id,
57	        severity=severity,
58	        result=result,
59	        reason=f"per AGENTS.md {rule_id}",
60	    )
61	
62	
63	def _has_relative_import(added: list[str]) -> bool:
64	    return any(re.search(r"^\s*from\s+\.", line) for line in added)
65	
66	
67	def _has_shell_execution(added: list[str]) -> bool:
68	    pattern = re.compile(r"\b(os\.system|subprocess(?:\.|\b))")
69	    return any(pattern.search(line) for line in added)
70	
71	
72	def _has_public_function_without_docstring(added: list[str]) -> bool:
73	    for index, line in enumerate(added):
74	        has_missing_docstring = not _next_added_line_is_docstring(added, index)
75	        if _is_public_function(line) and has_missing_docstring:
76	            return True
77	    return False
78	
79	
80	def _is_public_function(line: str) -> bool:
81	    return re.search(r"^\s*def\s+(?!_)[A-Za-z_]\w*\s*\(", line) is not None
82	
83	
84	def _next_added_line_is_docstring(added: list[str], index: int) -> bool:
85	    for line in added[index + 1 :]:
86	        stripped = line.strip()
87	        if not stripped:
88	            continue
89	        return stripped.startswith(('"""', "'''"))
90	    return False
91	
92	
93	def _has_print_call(added: list[str]) -> bool:
94	    return any(re.search(r"(^|[^\w.])print\s*\(", line) for line in added)
95	
96	
97	def _has_requests_call(added: list[str]) -> bool:
98	    pattern = re.compile(r"\brequests\.(get|post|put|patch|delete|head|options)\s*\(")
99	    return any(pattern.search(line) for line in added)
100

from uuid import uuid4

from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

from src.main import app

client = TestClient(app)


def test_health() -> None:
    response = client.get("/health")

    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"status": "ok"}


def test_full_session_workflow_uses_required_response_shapes() -> None:
    session = _create_session()
    session_id = session["id"]

    plan_response = client.post(f"/sessions/{session_id}/plan")
    assert plan_response.status_code == 200
    plan = plan_response.json()
    assert len(plan) >= 1
    assert "patches" not in plan[0]

    patch_response = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session_id}/patches",
        json={"step_id": plan[0]["id"]},
    )
    assert patch_response.status_code == 200
    patch = patch_response.json()
    assert patch["step_id"] == plan[0]["id"]
    assert "checks" not in patch

    check_response = client.post(f"/patches/{patch['id']}/check")
    assert check_response.status_code == 200
    checks = check_response.json()
    assert len(checks) == 5
    assert {check["ruleId"] for check in checks} == {"R1", "R2", "R3", "R4", "R5"}

    get_response = client.get(f"/sessions/{session_id}")
    assert get_response.status_code == 200
    nested = get_response.json()
    assert nested["steps"][0]["patches"][0]["checks"] == checks


def test_create_session_returns_empty_steps_and_server_fields() -> None:
    session = _create_session()

    assert session["brand"] == "efood"
    assert session["steps"] == []
    assert "trace_id" in session
    assert "created_at" in session


def test_brand_outside_literal_returns_422() -> None:
    response = client.post(
        "/sessions",
        json={"title": "Title", "description": "Description", "brand": "unknown"},
    )

    assert response.status_code == 422


def test_missing_session_returns_404() -> None:
    response = client.get(f"/sessions/{uuid4()}")

    assert response.status_code == 404


def test_missing_step_returns_404() -> None:
    session = _create_session()
    response = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session['id']}/patches",
        json={"step_id": str(uuid4())},
    )

    assert response.status_code == 404


def test_missing_patch_returns_404() -> None:
    response = client.post(f"/patches/{uuid4()}/check")

    assert response.status_code == 404


def test_check_is_idempotent_and_overwrites_checks() -> None:
    session = _create_session()
    plan = client.post(f"/sessions/{session['id']}/plan").json()
    patch = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session['id']}/patches",
        json={"step_id": plan[0]["id"]},
    ).json()

    first = client.post(f"/patches/{patch['id']}/check").json()
    second = client.post(f"/patches/{patch['id']}/check").json()
    nested = client.get(f"/sessions/{session['id']}").json()

    assert second == first
    assert nested["steps"][0]["patches"][0]["checks"] == second


def _create_session() -> dict[str, object]:
    response = client.post(
        "/sessions",
        json={
            "title": "AI coding session",
            "description": "Implement deterministic efood guardrails",
            "brand": "efood",
        },
    )

    assert response.status_code == 200
    return response.json()

file.content:
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Literal
from uuid import UUID

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field

Brand = Literal["efood", "glovo", "talabat"]
Severity = Literal["BLOCK", "WARN", "INFO"]
CheckResult = Literal["pass", "fail"]


class SessionCreate(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand


class PatchCreate(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    step_id: UUID


class PlanStepInput(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    description: str
    target_files: list[str]


class PatchProposalInput(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    diff: str


class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]


class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    step_id: UUID
    diff: str
    created_at: datetime


class GuardrailCheck(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    ruleId: str
    severity: Severity
    result: CheckResult
    reason: str


class PatchProposal(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    step_id: UUID
    brand: Brand
    diff: str
    checks: list[GuardrailCheck] = Field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: datetime


class PlanStep(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]
    patches: list[PatchProposal] = Field(default_factory=list)


class Session(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand
    trace_id: UUID
    steps: list[PlanStep] = Field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: datetime

From: agentic-ides-hiring@deliveryhero.com
Subject: Take-home — Agentic IDE Session Backend (Mini)

Hi,

Thanks for moving forward with us. Below is a scoped technical exercise.
Budget: 90 minutes (we usually run a 4-hour version; this is a compressed
take-home for early-stage screening). Please send a GitHub repo link when done.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Background
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Our Agentic IDEs team is building backends that power AI-assisted coding
sessions across Delivery Hero's brands (efood, Glovo, Talabat, foodora,
foodpanda). Each brand maintains its own AGENTS.md — an "Engineering
Manifesto" that codifies what AI-generated code is allowed to look like.

We need a backend service that hosts these sessions: a developer asks for
a change, the LLM proposes a patch, our guardrails decide whether the
patch is safe to merge against the brand's AGENTS.md rules.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Task
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Build an HTTP service that exposes the following workflow for ONE brand:
efood. The service should:

1. POST /sessions
   - Create a session for a developer request (title, description, brand).

2. POST /sessions/{id}/plan
   - Given the session description, return a list of PlanStep
     (description + target files). The LLM proposes; you decide the
     contract.

3. POST /sessions/{id}/patches
   - Given a PlanStep, return a PatchProposal (a unified diff string).
     The LLM proposes; you decide the contract.

4. POST /sessions/{id}/patches/{patchId}/check
   - Run guardrails against the patch. Return a list of GuardrailCheck
     (ruleId, severity, result, reason). Guardrails MUST consult the
     brand's AGENTS.md.

5. GET /sessions/{id}
   - Return the full session state for a reviewing engineer.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Inputs we provide
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

(A) efood/AGENTS.md — five rules:
  R1. All Python imports must be absolute. (syntactic)
  R2. No direct calls to `os.system` or `subprocess` without explicit
      review. (security)
  R3. Public functions must have docstrings. (style)
  R4. Logging must use the `efood.logging` module — never `print`. (brand)
  R5. New external HTTP calls must go through `efood.http_client`. (brand)

(B) Sample PR diff to test against (sample_diff.patch):
  - Adds a new module `pricing/discount.py` with:
    * `from .utils import calc` (passes R1)
    * `def apply_discount(order, pct):` with docstring (passes R3)
    * `print(f"Discount applied: {pct}")` (VIOLATES R4)
    * `requests.get(provider_url)` (VIOLATES R5)

We expect at least R4 and R5 to flag.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
What we evaluate
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- A working repo (`how-to-run` in under a minute).
- A README that explains decisions, trade-offs, and what you didn't do.
- Code we can read on a phone screen — we don't grade line count.
- How you used AI to build this is part of the evaluation, not a secret.

We do NOT specify: storage, auth, error model, async vs. sync, how
strictly to enforce rules, what "severity" means, or how to mock the
LLM. Decide and explain.

Good luck.

---

Execute the Agentic IDE take-home assignment playbook.

## Context

- Current git status: !`git status --short`
- Current branch: !`git branch --show-current`
- Execution protocol: !`cat _shared/ASSIGNMENT_EXECUTION_PROTOCOL.md`
- Repository rules: !`cat _shared/AGENTS.md`

## Assignment Loading

`$ARGUMENTS` is the path to the assignment file (e.g. `/assignment case-01-session-second/assignment.md`).

- If `$ARGUMENTS` looks like a file path → read it: !`cat "$ARGUMENTS" 2>/dev/null || echo "⚠ File not found: $ARGUMENTS — paste assignment text below"`
- If `$ARGUMENTS` is empty → use default: !`cat case-01-session-backend/ASSIGNMENT.md 2>/dev/null || echo "⚠ No assignment file found. Re-run as: /assignment path/to/ASSIGNMENT.md"`

Whichever loads successfully is the authoritative assignment for this run.

## Your Task

1. Start with a todo list and keep it current.
2. Preserve the deterministic boundary: LLM proposes; schemas, routes, guardrails, and tests decide.
3. Keep P0/P1/P2 scope explicit. Do not add P2 features unless the user asks.
4. Use the Builder, Reviewer, and Tester agent definitions under `.claude/agents/` when delegating.
5. Never invent requirements. If the assignment is ambiguous, document the assumption in README.
6. Before declaring completion, run the **Phase 5 JD Alignment bash script** from the protocol —
   every `grep` and `python3` assertion must exit 0.
7. Before declaring completion, run the **Phase 6 Assignment Requirements Gate** from the protocol —
   read the loaded assignment, extract every endpoint/MUST/evaluation requirement, verify each one,
   and paste the full checkbox report. Every box must be `[x]` before submit.
8. End with: files changed, pytest result, Phase 5 gate output, Phase 6 gate report.
9. **Phase 7 — Evaluator's Eye View**: after Phase 6 passes, run `/evaluate` on the submission:
   ```bash
   REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
   cd "$REPO_ROOT/evaluator" && uv run evaluate --path "$REPO_ROOT/case-01-session-backend"
   ```
   Report total score, recommendation, and top 3 discussion questions.
   If any axis scores below 4, identify which phase should have caught the gap.

## Guardrails

- Do not post comments, open PRs, push, or alter remotes unless the user explicitly asks.
- Do not read `.entire/metadata/**`.
- Do not log, print, or commit secrets.
- Do not bypass `AGENTS.md`; brand rules must be loaded just-in-time where the protocol requires it.

content.content:
1	from uuid import UUID
2	
3	from src.models import GuardrailCheck, PatchProposal, PlanStep, Session
4	
5	_sessions: dict[UUID, Session] = {}
6	_patches: dict[UUID, PatchProposal] = {}
7	
8	
9	def clear() -> None:
10	    _sessions.clear()
11	    _patches.clear()
12	
13	
14	def save_session(session: Session) -> Session:
15	    _sessions[session.id] = session
16	    return session
17	
18	
19	def get_session(session_id: UUID) -> Session | None:
20	    return _sessions.get(session_id)
21	
22	
23	def save_steps(session_id: UUID, steps: list[PlanStep]) -> list[PlanStep] | None:
24	    session = get_session(session_id)
25	    if session is None:
26	        return None
27	    session.steps = steps
28	    return session.steps
29	
30	
31	def find_step(session_id: UUID, step_id: UUID) -> PlanStep | None:
32	    session = get_session(session_id)
33	    if session is None:
34	        return None
35	    return next((step for step in session.steps if step.id == step_id), None)
36	
37	
38	def save_patch(session_id: UUID, patch: PatchProposal) -> PatchProposal | None:
39	    step = find_step(session_id, patch.step_id)
40	    if step is None:
41	        return None
42	    step.patches.append(patch)
43	    _patches[patch.id] = patch
44	    return patch
45	
46	
47	def get_patch(patch_id: UUID) -> PatchProposal | None:
48	    return _patches.get(patch_id)
49	
50	
51	def save_checks(
52	    patch_id: UUID,
53	    checks: list[GuardrailCheck],
54	) -> list[GuardrailCheck] | None:
55	    patch = get_patch(patch_id)
56	    if patch is None:
57	        return None
58	    patch.checks = checks
59	    return patch.checks
60

from uuid import uuid4

from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

from src.main import app

client = TestClient(app)


def test_health() -> None:
    response = client.get("/health")

    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"status": "ok"}


def test_full_session_workflow_uses_spec_endpoint() -> None:
    session = _create_session()
    session_id = session["id"]

    plan_response = client.post(f"/sessions/{session_id}/plan")
    assert plan_response.status_code == 200
    plan = plan_response.json()
    assert len(plan) >= 1
    assert "patches" not in plan[0]

    patch_response = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session_id}/patches",
        json={"step_id": plan[0]["id"]},
    )
    assert patch_response.status_code == 200
    patch = patch_response.json()
    assert patch["step_id"] == plan[0]["id"]
    assert "checks" not in patch

    # spec endpoint: POST /sessions/{id}/patches/{patchId}/check
    check_response = client.post(f"/sessions/{session_id}/patches/{patch['id']}/check")
    assert check_response.status_code == 200
    checks = check_response.json()
    assert len(checks) == 5
    assert {check["ruleId"] for check in checks} == {"R1", "R2", "R3", "R4", "R5"}

    get_response = client.get(f"/sessions/{session_id}")
    assert get_response.status_code == 200
    nested = get_response.json()
    assert nested["steps"][0]["patches"][0]["checks"] == checks


def test_spec_endpoint_blocks_r4_and_r5_from_sample_diff() -> None:
    """Sample diff (print + requests.get) must produce R4=BLOCK and R5=BLOCK."""
    session = _create_session()
    session_id = session["id"]

    plan = client.post(f"/sessions/{session_id}/plan").json()
    patch = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session_id}/patches",
        json={"step_id": plan[0]["id"]},
    ).json()

    checks = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session_id}/patches/{patch['id']}/check"
    ).json()
    by_rule = {c["ruleId"]: c for c in checks}

    assert by_rule["R4"]["result"] == "fail"
    assert by_rule["R4"]["severity"] == "BLOCK"
    assert by_rule["R5"]["result"] == "fail"
    assert by_rule["R5"]["severity"] == "BLOCK"
    # apply_discount has a docstring → R3 passes
    assert by_rule["R3"]["result"] == "pass"
    # no os.system/subprocess → R2 passes
    assert by_rule["R2"]["result"] == "pass"


def test_spec_endpoint_rejects_patch_from_different_session() -> None:
    session_a = _create_session()
    session_b = _create_session()

    plan_a = client.post(f"/sessions/{session_a['id']}/plan").json()
    patch_a = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session_a['id']}/patches",
        json={"step_id": plan_a[0]["id"]},
    ).json()

    response = client.post(f"/sessions/{session_b['id']}/patches/{patch_a['id']}/check")
    assert response.status_code == 404


def test_create_session_returns_empty_steps_and_server_fields() -> None:
    session = _create_session()

    assert session["brand"] == "efood"
    assert session["steps"] == []
    assert "trace_id" in session
    assert "created_at" in session


def test_brand_outside_literal_returns_422() -> None:
    response = client.post(
        "/sessions",
        json={"title": "Title", "description": "Description", "brand": "unknown"},
    )

    assert response.status_code == 422


def test_missing_session_returns_404() -> None:
    response = client.get(f"/sessions/{uuid4()}")

    assert response.status_code == 404


def test_missing_step_returns_404() -> None:
    session = _create_session()
    response = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session['id']}/patches",
        json={"step_id": str(uuid4())},
    )

    assert response.status_code == 404


def test_missing_patch_returns_404() -> None:
    response = client.post(f"/patches/{uuid4()}/check")

    assert response.status_code == 404


def test_missing_patch_via_spec_endpoint_returns_404() -> None:
    session = _create_session()
    response = client.post(f"/sessions/{session['id']}/patches/{uuid4()}/check")

    assert response.status_code == 404


def test_check_is_idempotent_and_overwrites_checks() -> None:
    session = _create_session()
    plan = client.post(f"/sessions/{session['id']}/plan").json()
    patch = client.post(
        f"/sessions/{session['id']}/patches",
        json={"step_id": plan[0]["id"]},
    ).json()
    session_id = session["id"]

    first = client.post(f"/sessions/{session_id}/patches/{patch['id']}/check").json()
    second = client.post(f"/sessions/{session_id}/patches/{patch['id']}/check").json()
    nested = client.get(f"/sessions/{session_id}").json()

    assert second == first
    assert nested["steps"][0]["patches"][0]["checks"] == second


def _create_session() -> dict[str, object]:
    response = client.post(
        "/sessions",
        json={
            "title": "AI coding session",
            "description": "Implement deterministic efood guardrails",
            "brand": "efood",
        },
    )

    assert response.status_code == 200
    return response.json()

input.new_string:
## Your Task

1. Start with a todo list and keep it current.
2. Preserve the deterministic boundary: LLM proposes; schemas, routes, guardrails, and tests decide.
3. Keep P0/P1/P2 scope explicit. Do not add P2 features unless the user asks.
4. Use the Builder, Reviewer, and Tester agent definitions under `.claude/agents/` when delegating.
5. Never invent requirements. If the assignment is ambiguous, document the assumption in README.
6. Before declaring completion, run the **Phase 5 JD Alignment bash script** from the protocol —
   every `grep` and `python3` assertion must exit 0.
7. Before declaring completion, run the **Phase 6 Assignment Requirements Gate** from the protocol —
   read the loaded assignment, extract every endpoint/MUST/evaluation requirement, verify each one,
   and paste the full checkbox report. Every box must be `[x]` before submit.
8. End with: files changed, pytest result, Phase 5 gate output, Phase 6 gate report.
9. **Phase 7 — Evaluator's Eye View**: after Phase 6 passes, run `/evaluate` on the submission:
   ```bash
   REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
   cd "$REPO_ROOT/evaluator" && uv run evaluate --path "$REPO_ROOT/case-01-session-backend"
   ```
   Report total score, recommendation, and top 3 discussion questions.
   If any axis scores below 4, identify which phase should have caught the gap.

## Scoring Traps — check each before Phase 6

These are silent point-killers that look fine locally but fail the evaluator's first scan:

### 1. Spec endpoint must exist verbatim
The assignment specifies `POST /sessions/{id}/patches/{patchId}/check`. An evaluator checks this endpoint first.
- **Wrong**: `POST /patches/{patchId}/check` only — evaluator marks as missing, no partial credit
- **Right**: implement the spec endpoint with session ownership validation; keep the short form as an alias if desired
- Verify: `grep -r "sessions.*patches.*check" src/routes.py` must return a match

### 2. Sample diff must demonstrate R4/R5 BLOCK in E2E
The assignment provides a sample diff: `print(...)` violates R4, `requests.get(...)` violates R5.
- **Wrong**: mock diff that generates clean code — tests pass but prove nothing about the core requirement
- **Right**: mock diff matches the assignment sample diff; E2E test asserts `R4.result == "fail"` and `R5.result == "fail"` via `/check`
- Verify: at least one test name contains "r4" or "r5" and asserts `"BLOCK"` severity

### 3. AGENTS.md context must appear in BOTH prompts
The LLM "proposes within brand constraints" narrative only holds if the patch prompt also receives brand rules.
- **Wrong**: AGENTS.md in plan prompt only — patch LLM has no brand context
- **Right**: `_patch_prompt(step, brand)` reads AGENTS.md and includes it; `create_patch(step, brand)` signature reflects this
- Verify: `grep -n "agents" src/llm.py` shows AGENTS.md read in both plan and patch paths

### 4. Note the assignment self-contradiction in README
The assignment says `from .utils import calc` "passes R1" — but R1 requires absolute imports, and `from .utils` is a relative import. This is a contradiction.
- Document the decision: "We implement R1 as written in AGENTS.md (absolute imports required). The sample diff's `from .utils import calc` therefore fails R1 with WARN severity, contrary to the assignment's inline note."
- This shows you read carefully and made an explicit decision rather than being confused by it.

### 5. Session ownership validation on spec endpoint
`POST /sessions/{id}/patches/{patchId}/check` must verify the patch belongs to the session.
- A patch_id from session A used against session B must return 404
- This proves the endpoint is real, not just a URL alias

## Your Task

1. Start with a todo list and keep it current.
2. Preserve the deterministic boundary: LLM proposes; schemas, routes, guardrails, and tests decide.
3. Keep P0/P1/P2 scope explicit. Do not add P2 features unless the user asks.
4. Use the Builder, Reviewer, and Tester agent definitions under `.claude/agents/` when delegating.
5. Never invent requirements. If the assignment is ambiguous, document the assumption in README.
6. Before declaring completion, run the **Phase 5 JD Alignment bash script** from the protocol —
   every `grep` and `python3` assertion must exit 0.
7. Before declaring completion, run the **Phase 6 Assignment Requirements Gate** from the protocol —
   read the loaded assignment, extract every endpoint/MUST/evaluation requirement, verify each one,
   and paste the full checkbox report. Every box must be `[x]` before submit.
8. End with: files changed, pytest result, Phase 5 gate output, Phase 6 gate report.
9. **Phase 7 — Evaluator's Eye View**: after Phase 6 passes, run `/evaluate` on the submission:
   ```bash
   REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
   cd "$REPO_ROOT/evaluator" && uv run evaluate --path "$REPO_ROOT/case-01-session-backend"
   ```
   Report total score, recommendation, and top 3 discussion questions.
   If any axis scores below 4, identify which phase should have caught the gap.

## Scoring Traps — check each before Phase 6

These are silent point-killers that look fine locally but fail the evaluator's first scan:

### 1. Spec endpoint must exist verbatim
The assignment specifies `POST /sessions/{id}/patches/{patchId}/check`. An evaluator checks this endpoint first.
- **Wrong**: `POST /patches/{patchId}/check` only — evaluator marks as missing, no partial credit
- **Right**: implement the spec endpoint with session ownership validation; keep the short form as an alias if desired
- Verify: `grep -r "sessions.*patches.*check" src/routes.py` must return a match

### 2. Sample diff must demonstrate R4/R5 BLOCK in E2E
The assignment provides a sample diff: `print(...)` violates R4, `requests.get(...)` violates R5.
- **Wrong**: mock diff that generates clean code — tests pass but prove nothing about the core requirement
- **Right**: mock diff matches the assignment sample diff; E2E test asserts `R4.result == "fail"` and `R5.result == "fail"` via `/check`
- Verify: at least one test name contains "r4" or "r5" and asserts `"BLOCK"` severity

### 3. AGENTS.md context must appear in BOTH prompts
The LLM "proposes within brand constraints" narrative only holds if the patch prompt also receives brand rules.
- **Wrong**: AGENTS.md in plan prompt only — patch LLM has no brand context
- **Right**: `_patch_prompt(step, brand)` reads AGENTS.md and includes it; `create_patch(step, brand)` signature reflects this
- Verify: `grep -n "agents" src/llm.py` shows AGENTS.md read in both plan and patch paths

### 4. Note the assignment self-contradiction in README
The assignment says `from .utils import calc` "passes R1" — but R1 requires absolute imports, and `from .utils` is a relative import. This is a contradiction.
- Document the decision: "We implement R1 as written in AGENTS.md (absolute imports required). The sample diff's `from .utils import calc` therefore fails R1 with WARN severity, contrary to the assignment's inline note."
- This shows you read carefully and made an explicit decision rather than being confused by it.

### 5. Session ownership validation on spec endpoint
`POST /sessions/{id}/patches/{patchId}/check` must verify the patch belongs to the session.
- A patch_id from session A used against session B must return 404
- This proves the endpoint is real, not just a URL alias

content.text:
# Assignment Workflow

Feature/Task: **여기서 구현은 다 했는데 그 다음단계 진행해줘**

Four phases in sequence. Do not skip or reorder.

---

## Phase 0 — Load Stack Constraints

Before any design begins, detect the tech stack and load the relevant skill as active constraints.

**Detection**: read the assignment spec (if a file path is given) or infer from 여기서 구현은 다 했는데 그 다음단계 진행해줘.

If the project uses **FastAPI / Python backend**:
→ Read `~/.claude/commands/fastapi.md` in full.
→ All patterns in that file are now **mandatory constraints** for Phase 1 design and Phase 2 implementation.
→ Tell the user:

```
Stack detected: FastAPI + Python
Loaded constraints from /fastapi skill:
  ✓ 3-tier schema (Input / Out / Domain)
  ✓ api/ → service/ → crud/ layer separation
  ✓ Annotated dependency aliases in deps.py
  ✓ Pydantic v2 (ConfigDict, model_dump, model_validate)
  ✓ lifespan instead of on_event
  ✓ Domain exceptions, not HTTPException in service layer

이 제약들이 Phase 1 설계와 Phase 2 구현에 적용돼.
```

If other stacks are detected (Next.js, Go, etc.) — note them and proceed without FastAPI constraints.

---

## Phase 1 — Think (Design)

Run the full `/think` flow for: 여기서 구현은 다 했는데 그 다음단계 진행해줘

Walk through all 8 stages (Stage 0 → Stage 7):
- Stage 0: 첫 궁금증 (5 first curiosity questions)
- Stage 1: 문제 이해
- Stage 2: AI 탐색 설계
- Stage 3: 결과 검증
- Stage 4: Trade-off 판단
- Stage 5: Edge Case 발견
- Stage 6: 방향 수정
- Stage 7: 최종 결정

At each stage: narrate the expert inner monologue → ask sharp questions → wait for user response → validate and move on.

**FastAPI-specific probes** (add these at the relevant stages if stack is FastAPI):

- Stage 0: "이 엔드포인트가 `service/`를 거쳐야 해, 아니면 `crud/`를 직접 불러도 돼?"
- Stage 1: "응답 schema가 몇 tier 필요해? Input / Out만? 아니면 DB domain model도?"
- Stage 4: "route → service → crud 레이어를 지키면 뭘 포기하게 돼? 그게 합리적이야?"
- Stage 5: "DB 세션 커밋/롤백이 어느 레이어 책임이야? `crud`가 commit하면 안 되는 이유는?"

At Stage 7, after the user writes the final decision sentence, delete any existing `/tmp/spec.md` then write a fresh one:

```markdown
# Implementation Spec — [feature name]

## Problem
[from Stage 0: who uses it, failure cost, scope boundary]

## Decision
[Stage 7 final decision sentence]

## Why
[Stage 4 trade-off rationale]

## Edge Cases to Handle
[Stage 5 findings]

## What NOT to build
[Stage 0 out-of-scope items]

## Implementation Constraints
[all constraints from stages 1–6]

### Code Style (FastAPI — mandatory if stack detected in Phase 0)
- Project structure: api/ → service/ → crud/ (routes never call crud directly)
- Schemas: 3 tiers — {Feature}Create (input), {Feature}Out (response), SQLAlchemy model (DB)
- Dependencies: Annotated aliases in api/deps.py — DBSession, CurrentUser
- Pydantic v2: ConfigDict(from_attributes=True), model_dump(), model_validate()
- App factory: lifespan context manager, not @app.on_event
- Errors: domain exceptions in service/, handler registered in main.py
- No HTTPException inside service layer
- response_model= explicit when return type differs from serialized type

## Files to touch
[expected file list following fastapi structure:
 api/v1/endpoints/, service/, crud/, models/, schemas/, core/config.py]
```

Then show the spec as a human-readable design summary — not the raw markdown, but a structured explanation:

```
## 설계 요약

### 뭘 만드나
[한 줄 — 서비스가 하는 일]

### 엔드포인트
[URL과 각각 한 줄 설명]

### 핵심 결정
[Stage 4의 옵션 비교 중 가장 중요한 것 2~3개]
- Option A vs B: [뭘 선택했고 왜, 재고 조건]

### 내가 가정하고 있는 것들
[설계에 묵시적으로 깔린 가정 — 지금 고쳐줘, 아니면 그대로 진행할게]
- [가정 1]
- [가정 2]

### 안 만드는 것
[What NOT to Build 항목 — 왜 제외했는지 한 줄씩]

### 파일 구조
[Files to Create 트리]
```

Then ask:

```
설계 리뷰해줘. 가정 중 틀린 거 있으면 지금 말해줘. 없으면 "응" 하면 구현 시작할게.
```

Wait for confirmation or adjustments before Phase 2. If adjustments: update `/tmp/spec.md` and re-show the summary.

Once confirmed, run the spec validator before handing off to Codex:

Spawn a subagent with `~/.claude/agents/spec-validator.md` as prompt.
- If STATUS is NEEDS UPDATE: apply the exact additions the validator provides to `/tmp/spec.md`, show the user what was added, then proceed.
- If STATUS is READY: proceed immediately.

---

## Phase 2 — Iterate (Implementation)

Run `/iterate` for each feature/task identified in Phase 1.

If spec covers a single feature:
```bash
{ cat /tmp/spec.md; echo -e "\nAfter implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/reply.txt
```

If spec covers multiple features, run `/iterate <feature>` for each one in priority order, showing the user the result between each and asking to continue.

After all iterations complete, proceed to Phase 3.

---

## Phase 3 — README Polish (Codex)

Run Codex to update README.md from the actual code and spec. **DO NOT edit README yourself.**

```bash
WORKTREE=$(cat /tmp/codex-worktree.txt 2>/dev/null || echo ".")
PROJECT_SUBDIR="<from spec>"
cd "$WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR"

{ cat /tmp/spec.md; echo "---"; cat README.md; echo -e "\n
Update README.md so that every section is accurate. Requirements:
1. Architecture diagram URLs must match actual @router. decorators exactly
2. Remove any claims (validate X, check Y) that don't exist in the code
3. Response shapes must match actual response_model fields
4. Trade-offs table must include every Stage 4 decision from the spec
5. AI Leverage table must list every file changed in Phase 2 with how it was verified
6. §If More Time must list every item from ## What NOT to Build in the spec
Write a one-line summary of changes to /tmp/readme-changes.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/readme-changes.txt
```

After Codex completes, proceed to Phase 4.

---

## Phase 4 — Final Gate (must all pass before commit)

First, detect the source root: check if `src/` or `app/` exists and use that as `$SRC`.

Run these and show each result:

```bash
SRC=$([ -d src ] && echo src || echo app)
uv run pytest -v
uv run ruff check $SRC/
```

| Check | Command | Must pass |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| LLM isolation | `grep -n "patch\|MagicMock" tests/conftest.py` | `patch("src.llm.anthropic.Anthropic")` in an `autouse=True` fixture — env var alone is not enough |
| Tests green | `uv run pytest -v` | 0 failed |
| Lint clean | `uv run ruff check $SRC/` | no output |
| Layer separation | `grep -rn "from $SRC.store\|from $SRC.crud" $SRC/routes.py 2>/dev/null` | no direct store/crud import in routes |
| URLs match README | `grep -rn "@router\." $SRC/` | matches architecture diagram |
| No false claims | read README §Architecture | every claim exists in code |
| Response shapes | read README §Architecture | matches response_model fields |
| Trade-offs complete | read README §4 | every Stage 4 decision documented |
| AI Leverage complete | read README §3 | every Phase 2 file listed |

Show result as:

```
Phase 4 — Final Gate

✓ LLM isolation: conftest.py sets ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="" before local imports
✓ pytest: N passed
✓ ruff: clean
✓ Layer separation: routes don't import crud directly
✓ README URLs consistent
✓ No false claims
✓ Response shapes accurate
✓ Trade-offs documented
✓ AI Leverage complete

Ready to commit. 커밋 메시지 확인 후 push.
```

If any check fails: fix it before showing this summary. Do not mark ✓ for a check you haven't run.

---

### Phase 4B — Spec Adherence (Evaluator Agent)

Runs only after **all mechanical checks above pass**.

Spawn a bounded evaluator subagent with this exact prompt:

```
Read /tmp/spec.md and the output of `git diff main` (run it first).

Return a scored checklist. For each item, mark PASS or FAIL and cite file:line if PASS.

Checklist:
1. Each ## Decision item — is it implemented?
2. Each ## Edge Cases to Handle item — is it handled?
3. Layer separation — no `from.*crud` imports in routes.py or api/ files
4. Response schemas omit child lists (PlanStepOut has no patches, PatchProposalOut has no checks)
5. LLM input schemas are not domain models (PlanStepInput / PatchProposalInput used, not PlanStep / PatchProposal)
6. Every GuardrailCheck.reason cites a specific rule ("per AGENTS.md R4", not generic "error")

Output format:
  ✓ [item] — [file:line]
  ✗ [item] — NOT found / describe gap
```

If any ✗ in the evaluator output → fix the gap, re-run mechanical checks, re-run evaluator.
Only proceed to commit when evaluator returns all ✓.

구현 완료. Phase 3 (README Polish) → Phase 4 (Final Gate) 순서로 진행합니다.

README 먼저 읽겠습니다.

content.content:
1	# efood AI Coding Session Guardrails
2	
3	> FastAPI service that hosts efood AI coding sessions, asks Claude only for plan and patch proposals, and runs deterministic R1-R5 guardrails before merge review.
4	>
5	> It is not a CI runner, auth layer, queue, or persistent patch database. It is the deterministic policy gate around LLM-generated coding diffs.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Problem & Approach
10	
11	**What this replaces**: a manual workflow where a developer asks an AI for a plan or diff, then reviewers have to remember and consistently apply efood's AGENTS.md rules before risky code reaches production.
12	
13	**Architecture**:
14	
15	```
16	[Request]
17	    |
18	    v
19	[POST /sessions]
20	  create Session with server-side id, trace_id, created_at, steps=[]      <- deterministic
21	
22	[POST /sessions/{session_id}/plan]
23	  load Session by UUID                                                    <- deterministic
24	  -> Claude tool_use output: list of plan steps                           <- non-deterministic, schema-constrained
25	  -> Pydantic validates PlanStepInput, store saves PlanStep objects       <- deterministic
26	
27	[POST /sessions/{session_id}/patches]
28	  load Session and step_id                                                <- deterministic
29	  -> Claude tool_use output: unified diff                                 <- non-deterministic, schema-constrained
30	  -> Pydantic validates PatchProposalInput, store saves PatchProposal     <- deterministic
31	
32	[POST /patches/{patch_id}/check]
33	  load PatchProposal by globally unique patch_id                          <- deterministic
34	  -> regex guardrails inspect added diff lines only                       <- deterministic
35	  -> store overwrites checks on each call                                 <- deterministic
36	
37	[GET /sessions/{session_id}]
38	  -> full state: Session -> steps -> patches -> checks
39	```
40	
41	The LLM proposes work; deterministic Python decides whether the proposed patch passes, warns, or blocks under R1-R5.
42	
43	**Actual endpoints**:
44	
45	| Method | Path | Response model |
46	|--------|------|----------------|
47	| `GET` | `/health` | `dict[str, str]` |
48	| `POST` | `/sessions` | `Session` |
49	| `POST` | `/sessions/{session_id}/plan` | `list[PlanStepOut]` |
50	| `POST` | `/sessions/{session_id}/patches` | `PatchProposalOut` |
51	| `POST` | `/patches/{patch_id}/check` | `list[GuardrailCheck]` |
52	| `GET` | `/sessions/{session_id}` | `Session` |
53	
54	**Assumptions**:
55	1. Storage is process-local memory in `src/store.py`; restart clears sessions and patches.
56	2. efood policy lives in `efood/AGENTS.md` and is implemented as regex checks in `src/guardrails.py`.
57	3. `BLOCK` means a merge gate must fail, `WARN` means reviewer attention is required, and `INFO` is available in the schema but not currently emitted by R1-R5.
58	4. LLM output is accepted only through Anthropic `tool_use` and Pydantic validation; domain models are not passed to the LLM.
59	5. `brand` accepts `efood`, `glovo`, or `talabat` at the schema layer, but only the efood rules are implemented.
60	6. `trace_id` is generated and returned on `Session`; there is no OTEL or distributed tracing.
61	7. If `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is absent, `src/llm.py` returns deterministic mock plan and patch data for tests and local demos.
62	
63	**Ambiguities I noticed**:
64	1. The schema accepts `glovo` and `talabat`, but the spec says not to build multi-brand routing; this implementation stores the brand and applies the same efood guardrails.
65	2. R3 defines "public function without docstring" for added lines only; this implementation checks whether the next non-empty added line after `def name(...)` starts with a triple-quoted docstring.
66	3. The service returns guardrail checks, not a separate merge decision object; downstream callers must interpret any `BLOCK` failure as not mergeable.
67	
68	---
69	
70	## 2. Domain Model
71	
72	- `Session` - one AI coding session with title, description, brand, trace_id, steps, and created_at.
73	- `PlanStep` - one LLM-proposed implementation step with target files and nested patches.
74	- `PatchProposal` - one LLM-proposed unified diff tied to a plan step and brand.
75	- `GuardrailCheck` - one deterministic R1-R5 result, including pass results as well as failures.
76	
77	```
78	Session 1--* PlanStep 1--* PatchProposal 1--* GuardrailCheck
79	```
80	
81	Severity ladder: `BLOCK` > `WARN` > `INFO`.
82	The check output is intended to feed a merge gate or review dashboard that can block on failed `BLOCK` rules.
83	
84	---
85	
86	## 3. Key Design Decisions
87	
88	The hardest part of this problem was keeping LLM usefulness without letting the LLM decide policy.
89	
90	### Guardrail Enforcement
91	
92	**Option 1 - Ask the LLM to review patches**
93	The same model that proposes a diff also judges whether it follows R1-R5.
94	Risk: identical patches can receive different judgments, which is unacceptable for a merge gate.
95	
96	**Option 2 - Deterministic regex checks on full diff text**
97	Apply regex rules across the whole patch.
98	Risk: removed lines, file headers, or context lines can create false failures.
99	
100	**Option 3 - Deterministic regex checks on added lines only**
101	Inspect lines that start with `+` while excluding `+++` diff headers.
102	Risk: complex Python semantics are not modeled, but the specified R1-R5 patterns are covered directly.
103	
104	**I chose Option 3** because it gives stable, repeatable results for the exact policy surface in the spec. The trade-off is that this is a guardrail scanner, not a general static analyzer. I would reconsider if AGENTS.md gained semantic rules that require parsing Python ASTs or cross-file context.
105	
106	### Patch Check Scope
107	
108	**Option 1 - `/sessions/{session_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check`**
109	Scope checks under sessions.
110	Risk: callers must carry redundant IDs even though patch IDs are UUIDs.
111	
112	**Option 2 - `/patches/{patch_id}/check`**
113	Check by globally unique patch ID.
114	Risk: the URL does not visibly show the parent session.
115	
116	**Option 3 - Compute checks during patch creation**
117	Always run guardrails inside `POST /sessions/{session_id}/patches`.
118	Risk: callers cannot explicitly re-run checks, and idempotent overwrite behavior is harder to demonstrate.
119	
120	**I chose Option 2** because it matches the implementation spec and keeps patch checks simple. The trade-off is less hierarchical routing, which is acceptable because the patch ID is globally unique.
121	
122	---
123	
124	## 4. Trade-offs & Decisions
125	
126	| Decision | Rationale | Reconsider if |
127	|----------|-----------|---------------|
128	| In-memory `dict` storage | Keeps the assignment focused on API shape, LLM contract, and guardrails. | Sessions must survive process restart or run across multiple workers. |
129	| Deterministic regex guardrails | R1-R5 are explicit string patterns, so deterministic checks are more reliable than LLM review. | Rules require Python AST analysis, dataflow, or repo-wide context. |
130	| LLM only for plan and patch generation | Keeps creative generation separate from merge policy. | The product needs natural-language explanation after deterministic checks complete. |
131	| `/patches/{patch_id}/check` URL | Patch UUIDs are globally unique, so session scope is redundant for the check call. | Patch IDs become scoped or human-readable rather than globally unique. |
132	| Store all five check results | Callers can see pass and fail outcomes for every R1-R5 rule. | Payload size becomes an issue after adding many more rules. |
133	| Idempotent check overwrite | Re-running `/check` replaces prior checks for the same patch. | Historical guardrail runs need audit retention. |
134	| No authentication or authorization | Explicitly out of scope for this service. | The API is exposed outside local or trusted test environments. |
135	| No DB, Redis, or persistence | Explicitly out of scope and unnecessary for the in-memory demo. | Production needs durability, concurrency, or audit trails. |
136	| No multi-brand routing | `brand` is stored, but only efood rules are implemented. | Glovo or talabat require different rule files or severity policies. |
137	| No async queue or streaming LLM | Requests are synchronous and simple to test. | Patch generation becomes slow or needs progress updates. |
138	| No OTEL or distributed tracing | Only a `trace_id` field exists today. | The service participates in production request tracing. |
139	| Direct Anthropic SDK with `tool_use` | It directly satisfies `tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}` and `max_tokens=4096`. | Multiple providers or richer orchestration become required. |
140	
141	---
142	
143	## 5. Edge Cases Considered
144	
145	- Missing session ID returns `404`.
146	- Missing step ID inside an existing session returns `404`.
147	- Missing patch ID returns `404`.
148	- `/patches/{patch_id}/check` can be called repeatedly and overwrites `checks`.
149	- Unknown `brand` values fail at Pydantic request validation with `422`.
150	- Missing `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` uses deterministic mock LLM output.
151	- Empty patch text returns five passing checks.
152	- Removed lines and diff headers are ignored because guardrails inspect added lines only.
153	
154	For the most critical case, guardrail determinism is handled by never asking the LLM to judge policy. `src/guardrails.py` returns the same five checks for the same diff every time.
155	
156	---
157	
158	## 6. AI Usage Log
159	
160	I used AI as a reasoning partner and implementation assistant. The implementation was checked against the spec, route decorators, response models, and tests.
161	
162	Example prompts used during design:
163	- "How should the service separate LLM patch generation from deterministic policy checks?"
164	- "What edge cases should the FastAPI layer handle for missing sessions, steps, and patches?"
165	- "How can R1-R5 be implemented using only added lines in a unified diff?"
166	- "Review the README against `src/routes.py` so documented URLs match actual decorators."
167	
168	Every implementation file was verified with `uv run ruff check`, `uv run ruff format --check`, and `uv run pytest`.
169	
170	| Part | Done by | Verification |
171	|------|---------|--------------|
172	| `pyproject.toml` | AI-assisted | Dependency/import validation through `uv run pytest`; style through `uv run ruff check`. |
173	| `src/main.py` | AI-assisted | `GET /health` test and route import through FastAPI `TestClient`. |
174	| `src/models.py` | AI-assisted | Response-shape tests and Pydantic validation in `tests/test_e2e.py`. |
175	| `src/store.py` | AI-assisted | Nested session workflow and idempotent check test. |
176	| `src/routes.py` | AI-assisted | Endpoint coverage in `tests/test_e2e.py`; README URLs grepped from decorators. |
177	| `src/service.py` | AI-assisted | Full session workflow, 404 behavior, and check overwrite tests. |
178	| `src/llm.py` | AI-assisted | Mock-mode workflow tests without `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`; tool-use contract checked by code review. |
179	| `src/guardrails.py` | AI-assisted | R1-R5 unit tests in `tests/test_guardrails.py`. |
180	| `tests/conftest.py` | AI-assisted | Store isolation confirmed by full pytest run. |
181	| `tests/test_guardrails.py` | AI-assisted | Exercises empty patch, added-line filtering, and each R1-R5 rule. |
182	| `tests/test_e2e.py` | AI-assisted | Exercises health, response shapes, validation, 404s, and idempotent checks. |
183	| `efood/AGENTS.md` | AI-assisted | Rule text cross-checked against `src/guardrails.py` and tests. |
184	| `README.md` | AI-assisted | Cross-checked against route decorators, models, tests, and spec exclusions. |
185	
186	---
187	
188	## 7. If More Time
189	
190	- **Authentication and authorization** -> add caller identity, session ownership, and permission checks before exposing this API beyond trusted local use.
191	- **Persistence with DB or Redis** -> replace process-local dictionaries with durable storage and indexes for sessions, steps, patches, and check history.
192	- **Multi-brand routing** -> keep the existing `brand` field, add per-brand rule configuration, and route `glovo` and `talabat` to their own policies.
193	- **Async queue and streaming LLM** -> move slow plan/patch generation into background jobs and stream status or partial output.
194	- **OTEL and distributed tracing** -> export `trace_id` into spans and logs so guardrail outcomes can be followed across services.
195	
196	---
197	
198	## How to Run
199	
200	```bash
201	uv sync
202	# Optional: set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in your environment for real Claude calls.
203	# Without it, the service uses deterministic mock LLM responses.
204	uv run pytest
205	uv run uvicorn src.main:app --reload
206	# -> http://localhost:8000/docs
207	```
208	
209	Healthcheck:
210	
211	```bash
212	curl localhost:8000/health
213	# {"status":"ok"}
214	```
215	
216	Full workflow:
217	
218	```bash
219	# 1. Create a session.
220	SESSION_ID=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions \
221	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
222	  -d '{"title":"AI coding session","description":"Implement deterministic efood guardrails","brand":"efood"}' \
223	  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")
224	
225	# 2. Create plan steps for the session.
226	STEP_ID=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION_ID/plan \
227	  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['id'])")
228	
229	# 3. Create a patch for one step.
230	PATCH_ID=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION_ID/patches \
231	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
232	  -d "{\"step_id\":\"$STEP_ID\"}" \
233	  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")
234	
235	# 4. Run deterministic guardrails.
236	curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/patches/$PATCH_ID/check | python3 -m json.tool
237	
238	# 5. Read full nested session state.
239	curl -s localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION_ID | python3 -m json.tool
240	```
241	
242	## Tested Working
243	
244	- `GET /health` -> `{"status":"ok"}`.
245	- `POST /sessions` -> `Session` with `steps=[]`, server-generated `id`, `trace_id`, and `created_at`.
246	- `POST /sessions/{session_id}/plan` -> `list[PlanStepOut]` with no `patches` field.
247	- `POST /sessions/{session_id}/patches` -> `PatchProposalOut` with no `checks` field.
248	- `POST /patches/{patch_id}/check` -> five `GuardrailCheck` objects for R1-R5, including pass results.
249	- `GET /sessions/{session_id}` -> nested `Session` with `steps -> patches -> checks`.
250

file.content:
# efood AI Coding Session Guardrails

> FastAPI service that hosts efood AI coding sessions, asks Claude only for plan and patch proposals, and runs deterministic R1-R5 guardrails before merge review.
>
> It is not a CI runner, auth layer, queue, or persistent patch database. It is the deterministic policy gate around LLM-generated coding diffs.

---

## 1. Problem & Approach

**What this replaces**: a manual workflow where a developer asks an AI for a plan or diff, then reviewers have to remember and consistently apply efood's AGENTS.md rules before risky code reaches production.

**Architecture**:

```
[Request]
    |
    v
[POST /sessions]
  create Session with server-side id, trace_id, created_at, steps=[]      <- deterministic

[POST /sessions/{session_id}/plan]
  load Session by UUID                                                    <- deterministic
  -> Claude tool_use output: list of plan steps                           <- non-deterministic, schema-constrained
  -> Pydantic validates PlanStepInput, store saves PlanStep objects       <- deterministic

[POST /sessions/{session_id}/patches]
  load Session and step_id                                                <- deterministic
  -> Claude tool_use output: unified diff                                 <- non-deterministic, schema-constrained
  -> Pydantic validates PatchProposalInput, store saves PatchProposal     <- deterministic

[POST /patches/{patch_id}/check]
  load PatchProposal by globally unique patch_id                          <- deterministic
  -> regex guardrails inspect added diff lines only                       <- deterministic
  -> store overwrites checks on each call                                 <- deterministic

[GET /sessions/{session_id}]
  -> full state: Session -> steps -> patches -> checks
```

The LLM proposes work; deterministic Python decides whether the proposed patch passes, warns, or blocks under R1-R5.

**Actual endpoints**:

| Method | Path | Response model |
|--------|------|----------------|
| `GET` | `/health` | `dict[str, str]` |
| `POST` | `/sessions` | `Session` |
| `POST` | `/sessions/{session_id}/plan` | `list[PlanStepOut]` |
| `POST` | `/sessions/{session_id}/patches` | `PatchProposalOut` |
| `POST` | `/patches/{patch_id}/check` | `list[GuardrailCheck]` |
| `GET` | `/sessions/{session_id}` | `Session` |

**Assumptions**:
1. Storage is process-local memory in `src/store.py`; restart clears sessions and patches.
2. efood policy lives in `efood/AGENTS.md` and is implemented as regex checks in `src/guardrails.py`.
3. `BLOCK` means a merge gate must fail, `WARN` means reviewer attention is required, and `INFO` is available in the schema but not currently emitted by R1-R5.
4. LLM output is accepted only through Anthropic `tool_use` and Pydantic validation; domain models are not passed to the LLM.
5. `brand` accepts `efood`, `glovo`, or `talabat` at the schema layer, but only the efood rules are implemented.
6. `trace_id` is generated and returned on `Session`; there is no OTEL or distributed tracing.
7. If `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is absent, `src/llm.py` returns deterministic mock plan and patch data for tests and local demos.

**Ambiguities I noticed**:
1. The schema accepts `glovo` and `talabat`, but the spec says not to build multi-brand routing; this implementation stores the brand and applies the same efood guardrails.
2. R3 defines "public function without docstring" for added lines only; this implementation checks whether the next non-empty added line after `def name(...)` starts with a triple-quoted docstring.
3. The service returns guardrail checks, not a separate merge decision object; downstream callers must interpret any `BLOCK` failure as not mergeable.

---

## 2. Domain Model

- `Session` - one AI coding session with title, description, brand, trace_id, steps, and created_at.
- `PlanStep` - one LLM-proposed implementation step with target files and nested patches.
- `PatchProposal` - one LLM-proposed unified diff tied to a plan step and brand.
- `GuardrailCheck` - one deterministic R1-R5 result, including pass results as well as failures.

```
Session 1--* PlanStep 1--* PatchProposal 1--* GuardrailCheck
```

Severity ladder: `BLOCK` > `WARN` > `INFO`.
The check output is intended to feed a merge gate or review dashboard that can block on failed `BLOCK` rules.

---

## 3. Key Design Decisions

The hardest part of this problem was keeping LLM usefulness without letting the LLM decide policy.

### Guardrail Enforcement

**Option 1 - Ask the LLM to review patches**
The same model that proposes a diff also judges whether it follows R1-R5.
Risk: identical patches can receive different judgments, which is unacceptable for a merge gate.

**Option 2 - Deterministic regex checks on full diff text**
Apply regex rules across the whole patch.
Risk: removed lines, file headers, or context lines can create false failures.

**Option 3 - Deterministic regex checks on added lines only**
Inspect lines that start with `+` while excluding `+++` diff headers.
Risk: complex Python semantics are not modeled, but the specified R1-R5 patterns are covered directly.

**I chose Option 3** because it gives stable, repeatable results for the exact policy surface in the spec. The trade-off is that this is a guardrail scanner, not a general static analyzer. I would reconsider if AGENTS.md gained semantic rules that require parsing Python ASTs or cross-file context.

### Patch Check Scope

**Option 1 - `/sessions/{session_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check`**
Scope checks under sessions.
Risk: callers must carry redundant IDs even though patch IDs are UUIDs.

**Option 2 - `/patches/{patch_id}/check`**
Check by globally unique patch ID.
Risk: the URL does not visibly show the parent session.

**Option 3 - Compute checks during patch creation**
Always run guardrails inside `POST /sessions/{session_id}/patches`.
Risk: callers cannot explicitly re-run checks, and idempotent overwrite behavior is harder to demonstrate.

**I chose Option 2** because it matches the implementation spec and keeps patch checks simple. The trade-off is less hierarchical routing, which is acceptable because the patch ID is globally unique.

---

## 4. Trade-offs & Decisions

| Decision | Rationale | Reconsider if |
|----------|-----------|---------------|
| In-memory `dict` storage | Keeps the assignment focused on API shape, LLM contract, and guardrails. | Sessions must survive process restart or run across multiple workers. |
| Deterministic regex guardrails | R1-R5 are explicit string patterns, so deterministic checks are more reliable than LLM review. | Rules require Python AST analysis, dataflow, or repo-wide context. |
| LLM only for plan and patch generation | Keeps creative generation separate from merge policy. | The product needs natural-language explanation after deterministic checks complete. |
| `/patches/{patch_id}/check` URL | Patch UUIDs are globally unique, so session scope is redundant for the check call. | Patch IDs become scoped or human-readable rather than globally unique. |
| Store all five check results | Callers can see pass and fail outcomes for every R1-R5 rule. | Payload size becomes an issue after adding many more rules. |
| Idempotent check overwrite | Re-running `/check` replaces prior checks for the same patch. | Historical guardrail runs need audit retention. |
| No authentication or authorization | Explicitly out of scope for this service. | The API is exposed outside local or trusted test environments. |
| No DB, Redis, or persistence | Explicitly out of scope and unnecessary for the in-memory demo. | Production needs durability, concurrency, or audit trails. |
| No multi-brand routing | `brand` is stored, but only efood rules are implemented. | Glovo or talabat require different rule files or severity policies. |
| No async queue or streaming LLM | Requests are synchronous and simple to test. | Patch generation becomes slow or needs progress updates. |
| No OTEL or distributed tracing | Only a `trace_id` field exists today. | The service participates in production request tracing. |
| Direct Anthropic SDK with `tool_use` | It directly satisfies `tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}` and `max_tokens=4096`. | Multiple providers or richer orchestration become required. |

---

## 5. Edge Cases Considered

- Missing session ID returns `404`.
- Missing step ID inside an existing session returns `404`.
- Missing patch ID returns `404`.
- `/patches/{patch_id}/check` can be called repeatedly and overwrites `checks`.
- Unknown `brand` values fail at Pydantic request validation with `422`.
- Missing `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` uses deterministic mock LLM output.
- Empty patch text returns five passing checks.
- Removed lines and diff headers are ignored because guardrails inspect added lines only.

For the most critical case, guardrail determinism is handled by never asking the LLM to judge policy. `src/guardrails.py` returns the same five checks for the same diff every time.

---

## 6. AI Usage Log

I used AI as a reasoning partner and implementation assistant. The implementation was checked against the spec, route decorators, response models, and tests.

Example prompts used during design:
- "How should the service separate LLM patch generation from deterministic policy checks?"
- "What edge cases should the FastAPI layer handle for missing sessions, steps, and patches?"
- "How can R1-R5 be implemented using only added lines in a unified diff?"
- "Review the README against `src/routes.py` so documented URLs match actual decorators."

Every implementation file was verified with `uv run ruff check`, `uv run ruff format --check`, and `uv run pytest`.

| Part | Done by | Verification |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `pyproject.toml` | AI-assisted | Dependency/import validation through `uv run pytest`; style through `uv run ruff check`. |
| `src/main.py` | AI-assisted | `GET /health` test and route import through FastAPI `TestClient`. |
| `src/models.py` | AI-assisted | Response-shape tests and Pydantic validation in `tests/test_e2e.py`. |
| `src/store.py` | AI-assisted | Nested session workflow and idempotent check test. |
| `src/routes.py` | AI-assisted | Endpoint coverage in `tests/test_e2e.py`; README URLs grepped from decorators. |
| `src/service.py` | AI-assisted | Full session workflow, 404 behavior, and check overwrite tests. |
| `src/llm.py` | AI-assisted | Mock-mode workflow tests without `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`; tool-use contract checked by code review. |
| `src/guardrails.py` | AI-assisted | R1-R5 unit tests in `tests/test_guardrails.py`. |
| `tests/conftest.py` | AI-assisted | Store isolation confirmed by full pytest run. |
| `tests/test_guardrails.py` | AI-assisted | Exercises empty patch, added-line filtering, and each R1-R5 rule. |
| `tests/test_e2e.py` | AI-assisted | Exercises health, response shapes, validation, 404s, and idempotent checks. |
| `efood/AGENTS.md` | AI-assisted | Rule text cross-checked against `src/guardrails.py` and tests. |
| `README.md` | AI-assisted | Cross-checked against route decorators, models, tests, and spec exclusions. |

---

## 7. If More Time

- **Authentication and authorization** -> add caller identity, session ownership, and permission checks before exposing this API beyond trusted local use.
- **Persistence with DB or Redis** -> replace process-local dictionaries with durable storage and indexes for sessions, steps, patches, and check history.
- **Multi-brand routing** -> keep the existing `brand` field, add per-brand rule configuration, and route `glovo` and `talabat` to their own policies.
- **Async queue and streaming LLM** -> move slow plan/patch generation into background jobs and stream status or partial output.
- **OTEL and distributed tracing** -> export `trace_id` into spans and logs so guardrail outcomes can be followed across services.

---

## How to Run

```bash
uv sync
# Optional: set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in your environment for real Claude calls.
# Without it, the service uses deterministic mock LLM responses.
uv run pytest
uv run uvicorn src.main:app --reload
# -> http://localhost:8000/docs
```

Healthcheck:

```bash
curl localhost:8000/health
# {"status":"ok"}
```

Full workflow:

```bash
# 1. Create a session.
SESSION_ID=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"AI coding session","description":"Implement deterministic efood guardrails","brand":"efood"}' \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")

# 2. Create plan steps for the session.
STEP_ID=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION_ID/plan \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['id'])")

# 3. Create a patch for one step.
PATCH_ID=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION_ID/patches \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"step_id\":\"$STEP_ID\"}" \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")

# 4. Run deterministic guardrails.
curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/patches/$PATCH_ID/check | python3 -m json.tool

# 5. Read full nested session state.
curl -s localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION_ID | python3 -m json.tool
```

## Tested Working

- `GET /health` -> `{"status":"ok"}`.
- `POST /sessions` -> `Session` with `steps=[]`, server-generated `id`, `trace_id`, and `created_at`.
- `POST /sessions/{session_id}/plan` -> `list[PlanStepOut]` with no `patches` field.
- `POST /sessions/{session_id}/patches` -> `PatchProposalOut` with no `checks` field.
- `POST /patches/{patch_id}/check` -> five `GuardrailCheck` objects for R1-R5, including pass results.
- `GET /sessions/{session_id}` -> nested `Session` with `steps -> patches -> checks`.
